title: Log Proof
purpose: Verify behavior through current runtime logs while preserving direct, proxy, local, and blocked evidence boundaries.
trigger: Run when a task claims backend, worker, mobile, or device behavior that should emit logs.
allowedActions: collect logs for the requested time window
search for expected markers and errors
forbiddenActions: paste secrets or private tokens into reports
treat old unrelated logs as direct proof
claim success when only startup logs exist
gates: log source is current and timestamped
expected success marker or error marker is present
secret scan or manual redaction check completed

evidence.direct: Current runtime logs for the target action were collected and inspected.
evidence.proxy: Historical or adjacent logs support but do not prove the exact action.
evidence.local: Local fixture or unit logs only.
evidence.blocked: Logs unavailable, timestamps ambiguous, correlation id missing, or secrets cannot be safely redacted.

escalation: Stop if logs contain secrets that cannot be safely redacted.
Escalate when the expected marker is absent but the product behavior is ambiguous.
